data.title: European Commission finds Meta fails to keep minors off platform
data.description:
The European Union issued preliminary findings showing that Meta Platforms (META) violated its Digital Services Act by not preventing children under 13 from accessing the Facebook and Instagram platform.

According to their investigation, Meta’s (META) measures to confirm a user’s age “do not seem to be effective” and “do not adequately prevent minors under the age of 13 from accessing their services nor promptly identify and remove them.”

The EU’s Digital Services Act aims to protect minors from online grooming, harmful content, problematic and addictive behaviors, and online bullying. It requires platforms to set minor accounts to private by default, lower the risk of children encountering harmful content, and provide features that disable excessive use, among other features. Crucially, the Act requires “effective age assurance methods” that are “accurate, reliable, robust, non-intrusive, and non-discriminatory.”

The EU’s investigation into Meta (META) found that as minors can enter a false birth date, the platform does not have effective controls to check the accuracy of the self-declared birth date.

The Facebook and Instagram platforms also make it difficult to report minors under 13 and do not provide proper follow-up, enabling the minor to continue to use these platforms.

“Meta’s assessment contradicts large bodies of evidence from all over the European Union indicating that roughly 10-12% of children under 13 are accessing Instagram and/or Facebook. Moreover, Meta seems to have disregarded readily available scientific evidence indicating that younger children are more vulnerable to potential harms caused by services like Facebook and Instagram,” the Commission said in a statement.

While the Commission found Meta’s (META) tools inadequate, the company does employ several methods to identify underage users on its platforms. Using artificial intelligence, Meta (META) analyzes content interactions, profiles, and online behaviors to identify minors on the platform, as well as AI-based face recognition to verify age.

The company also prevents users over the age of 18 from starting private chats with minors that they are not connected to and restricts advertisers from targeting teens or minors.

In a statement to CNBC, Meta (META) said it “disagrees with these preliminary findings” and will continue to invest in technologies to find and remove underage users.”

“Understanding age is an industry-wide challenge, which requires an industry-wide solution, and we will continue to engage constructively with the European Commission on this important issue…we will have more to share next week about additional measures rolling out soon,” the company added.

If the Commission’s findings are confirmed, Meta (META) faces a fine proportionate to the infringement, though not to exceed 6% of the total worldwide annual turnover. Periodic penalties can also be imposed to compel the company to comply.

Seeking Alpha has reached out to Meta Platforms (META) for a response.

data.title: O'Keefe Stevens Advisory adds BAX, WY; exits BABA, MBGAF among Q1 moves
data.description:
• O'Keefe Stevens Advisory, during the first quarter of 2026, sold its position in Alibaba (BABA), Fannie Mae common, BMW (BMWKY), and Mercedes-Benz (MBGAF). The fund also closed its position in Tri Pointe Homes (TPH) due to the announced sale of the business.
  • The fund also trimmed its position in Corning during the quarter.
  • On the other hand, the fund initiated a new position in Baxter International (BAX) and Weyerhaeuser (WY).
  • The fund also strengthened its position in Perrigo (PRGO).
  • Source: Quarterly news letter

data.title: Virtu outlines hiring toward ~1,100 headcount while expanding trading capital base
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Virtu Financial (VIRT) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “Our first quarter results show that we're executing on our plan to grow through investing in our infrastructure, acquiring top talent and expanding our capital base.” (CEO & Director Aaron Simons)
  • “Following that plan in the last 7 months, we have added over $500 million in new trading capital and maintained a return on our total capital in excess of 100%.” (CEO & Director Simons)
  • “For the first quarter of 2026, we generated adjusted net trading income, or ANTI, of $12.9 million per day or a total of $787 million.” (Executive VP, CFO & Treasurer Cindy Lee)
  • “We generated $521 million in adjusted EBITDA, representing a 66% margin. Adjusted EPS was $2.24.” (CFO Lee)
  • “We will continue to expand our capital base, strengthening our infrastructure and deploy capital where we see the greatest opportunities, all while maintaining our quarterly dividend of $0.24 per share.” (CFO Lee)

Outlook

  • “We really don't do this month-to-month.” (Co-President & Co-COO Joseph Molluso)
  • “Just because it's more muted... doesn't mean it isn't a very good environment.” (Co-President & Co-COO Molluso)
  • “This year, we hope to get our headcount close to 1,100.” (CEO & Director Simons)
  • “Through the cycle, at the point, Patrick, is the key point in that discussion... when we talk about goals and trading capital of $4 billion, that factors into that goal.” (Co-President & Co-COO Molluso)

Financial Results

  • “This was the highest quarter total ever for Virtu.” (CFO Lee)
  • “Market Making reported ANTI of $10.4 million per day for Q1, Execution Services reached $2.5 million per day for the quarter.” (CFO Lee)
  • “Our first quarter 2026 cash compensation ratio was at 22%, which was within the historical range.” (CFO Lee)
  • “Our invested capital stands at $2.6 billion as of March 31, while generating an average return of 107% on the capital over the past year.” (CFO Lee)

Q&A

  • Patrick Moley, Piper Sandler: asked where Virtu saw the most opportunity and how to think about sustainability; Co-President & Co-COO Molluso said, “it's across the board” and “the growth plan isn't limited to a handful of narrow areas,” while CEO & Director Simons added, “the idea is that in any environment, we should outperform where we were before with lower capital.”
  • Daniel Fannon, Jefferies: asked about hiring focus and expense growth; CEO & Director Simons said Virtu is hiring “trader to quant to researcher” roles and “engineers, software developers,” and added, “for the foreseeable future, we're going to be pretty aggressively hiring,” while Co-President & Co-COO Molluso said if the comp ratio rises, “that will be deliberate and intentional.”
  • Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs: asked about divergence between trading results and trading costs plus early Q2 environment; Co-President & Co-COO Molluso said, “The answer is all of the above,” and cautioned, “I'm not sure I'd read anything permanent or long term into it,” while also pointing to Execution Services momentum: “there's client wins, there's multiple products kind of being tied together across clients.”
  • Kenneth Worthington, JPMorgan: asked about a “multiplier” from capital and new markets like prediction and tokenized markets; Co-President & Co-COO Molluso said, “I'm not sure there was any implication of a multiplier,” and CEO & Director Simons said on tokenization, “it really depends on volume.”
  • Michael Cyprys, Morgan Stanley: asked about Execution Services drivers and AI use; Co-President & Co-COO Molluso said VES was built through “a multiyear process” and that ITG moved from “a very siloed organization” to tied-together products and clients, while CEO & Director Simons said on AI, “it's a little early yet to determine the productivity impact, but I expect in the coming year or 2, it will have a material impact.”
  • Craig Siegenthaler, BofA Securities: asked if strong results reflected risk management changes and why Virtu doesn’t run a hedge fund; CEO & Director Simons said, “I don't think there's been any change in risk management,” and Co-President & Co-COO Molluso added, “the risk profile of the firm has not changed materially,” while Simons said a hedge fund would require “far lower sharp strategies” and “would just make the problem much worse.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly positive but probing on sustainability, cost drivers, and risk profile, including, “how should we think about the sustainability of that in this environment?” (Patrick Moley, Piper Sandler) and “any color... on how the environment is unfolding so far in the second quarter” (Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs).
  • Management tone was confident on execution but cautious on forecasting and near-term read-throughs, including, “the honest answer is we don't know” (CEO & Director Simons) and “I'm not sure I'd read anything permanent or long term into it” (Co-President & Co-COO Molluso).
  • Versus last quarter, management repeated the same growth framing (“investing in our infrastructure, acquiring talent and expanding our capital base”) while Q1 language leaned more heavily on record results and capital additions; analysts broadened into cost of trading dynamics, AI, and explicit risk-management questions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 emphasized record performance and scaling capital, with CFO Lee saying Q1 was “the highest quarter total ever,” compared with Q4’s framing of a growth “preview” as “capital accumulation efforts are just underway.” (CEO & Director Simons, prior quarter)
  • In Q4, CFO Lee reported ANTI of “$9.7 million per day... $613 million,” adjusted EBITDA of “$442 million,” and adjusted EPS of “$1.85,” while Q1’s prepared remarks highlighted higher ANTI, higher adjusted EBITDA, and higher adjusted EPS. (CFO Lee)
  • Analyst focus shifted from Q4’s repeated questions on equities vs. non-equities and capital deployment toward Q1 questions on sustainability through the cycle, execution-cost dynamics, AI productivity, and whether elevated results reflected higher risk-taking.

Risks and Concerns

  • “The environment... is the most important variable.” (CEO & Director Simons)
  • “In environments like this... we need much less and we make more than 10 million a day.” (CEO & Director Simons)
  • On AI tooling risk, CEO & Director Simons said, “introducing a bunch of technical [ debt ] of AI-generated slop is really never going to be in our business plan.”
  • On prediction markets, CEO & Director Simons said, “we don't have any expertise predicting like geopolitical events,” and added, “it's really about where the volume goes.”

Final Takeaway

Virtu described Q1 2026 as a record quarter supported by a favorable trading backdrop and a larger capital base, while reiterating a broad-based growth plan centered on infrastructure investment, aggressive hiring (with a goal to approach ~1,100 headcount), and continued capital expansion. Management repeatedly tied performance to market conditions and capital deployment rather than changes in risk posture, and positioned Execution Services as a multi-year growth engine with ongoing client penetration, while maintaining a cautious stance on short-term environment read-throughs and the pace of AI productivity benefits.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Medical Properties Trust Q1 earnings preview: What to expect
data.description:
Medical Properties Trust (MPT) is set to announce first-quarter earnings on Thursday, April 30th, before the market open.

Wall Street expects the hospital and medical facility REIT to post EPS of $0.01 on revenue of $249.68M for the quarter.

Last quarter, the REIT reported FFO of $0.18, beating expectations by $0.03, while revenue of $270.34M was $25.68M higher than market estimates.

During the firm’s Q4 earnings call, Rosa Hooper, Senior VP of Operations & Secretary at Medical Properties Trust, highlighted that the company expects 2026 to be a year of continued stabilization and increasing cash rents as our tenants capitalize on service line enhancements, reimbursement tailwinds, EMR modernization, and operating efficiencies gained throughout 2025.

Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish and rate, on average, MPT a Buy.

Seeking Alpha author Daniel Jones rated MPT a Strong Buy as operational recovery accelerates and tenant issues are largely resolved.

Jones added that MPT's re-tenanting efforts and new leases are driving rent growth, with annualized cash rent targeted at $1 billion by year-end.

Agreeing with Jones’ bullish views, Seeking Alpha analyst Juxtaposed Ideas added that despite uncertain macroeconomic risks, the REIT's improving portfolio and balance sheet performance signals resilience, aided by a secure AFFO payout ratio of 50%.

However, Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating are cautious, rating MPT a Hold and Sell, respectively.

The REIT’s shares have risen nearly 4.1% year-to-date, compared to the benchmark S&P 500’s 4.8% gain in the same period.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have not seen any upward revisions against one downward move, while revenue estimates have not seen any upward revisions compared to one downward revision.

data.title: F5 rises on Q2 performance; analysts point out AI revenue growth
data.description:
F5 (FFIV) shares climbed 7% during Wednesday morning trading after the application delivery and security company reported second-quarter fiscal 2026 results that surpassed expectations and increased its full-year revenue outlook.

Analysts were impressed by the revenue growth in Systems hardware, and they also noted more evidence of AI-related gains.

"Systems revenue of +26% Y/Y vs. MSe of +15% Y/Y was driven by multi-cloud, investments in digital sovereignty, particularly in EMEA, and AI (with the company noting ~$50mm from AI in the 1H—or ~3% of revenue, growing 200%+ Y/Y)," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Antonio Jaramillo in a Wednesday investor note.

Morgan Stanley maintained its Equal-weight rating and increased its price target to $340 from $305.

"Given margin headwinds from memory, uncertain dynamics around pullforward given upcoming pricing increases, and needing to know how reasonable we view company guidance at the upcoming Analyst Day, we are still on the sidelines for now," Marshall noted. "However, with software having the potential to accelerate as well and AI use cases having the potential to be $100mm+ this year, we see increasing reasons to be encouraged around the name."

Meanwhile, Needham maintained its Hold rating and did not set a price target.

"Management raised FY26 Revenue growth guidance to 7-8% from 5-6%, and signaled the possibility of Systems to grow in FY27," said Needham analysts Jeffrey Hopson and Ryan Koontz in a note. "However, despite F5 executing on its hardware refresh, we are still uncertain what Systems growth looks like after the refresh and are not yet convinced AI tailwinds can move the needle."

F5 plans to hold its Analyst Day event at 1 p.m. ET on Thursday, May 28, in New York City. It will be live streamed.

data.title: Amphenol surges after strong Q1 results, guidance
data.description:
Amphenol (APH) shares surged more than 5% on Wednesday after the electronic connector manufacturer reported stronger-than-expected results and guidance.

For the first quarter, which ended March 31, Amphenol said it earned an adjusted $1.06 per share as revenue rose 58.4% year-over-year to come in at $7.62B. Analysts had expected the company to earn $0.94 per share on $7.08B in revenue.

Adjusted operating margin during the period was 27.3%, while operating and free cash flow came in at $1.1B and $831M, respectively.

“We are pleased to have closed the first quarter of 2026 with record sales and Adjusted Diluted EPS, both exceeding the high end of our guidance,” said Amphenol President and CEO R. Adam Norwitt in a statement. “Sales increased from prior year by 58%, driven by strong organic growth in most of our end markets, including exceptional organic growth in the IT datacom market, as well as contributions from the company's acquisition program. We also booked record orders in the quarter, resulting in a book-to-bill of 1.24:1. The Company realized strong profitability during the quarter with adjusted operating margin reaching 27.3%. We are extremely proud of the company's outstanding performance.”

BNP Paribas analyst Andrew Buscaglia said the results were “strong,” especially in its Communications Solutions segment, which had “impressive” 47% growth. 

“Overall, we believe APH’s results and Q2 outlook demonstrate continued strength in AI data centers, despite beginning to lap difficult comps,” Buscaglia wrote in a note to clients.

Looking to the second-quarter, Amphenol expects to earn between $1.14 and $1.16 per share on an adjusted basis, ahead of the $1.05 analysts had expected. Sales are forecast to be between $8.1B and $8.2B, above the $7.7B estimate. 

The company will host a conference call at 1 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

(This story has been updated to include analyst commentary.)

data.title: SNDL sends pot stocks lower as market headwinds drive Q1 revenue miss
data.description:
SNDL (SNDL) led decliners among Canadian cannabis firms on Wednesday after announcing lower-than-expected Q1 2026 revenue, which the company attributed to a sales decline driven by market headwinds.

The Edmonton-based licensed producer lost ~9% in the morning hours, while its Canadian peers, including Tilray (TLRY), Canopy Growth (CGC), and Aurora Cannabis (ACB), were among notable decliners.

SNDL generated C$195.9M in revenue during the quarter, with a ~4% YoY decline marking its first topline contraction in three quarters.

SNDL’s net revenue from cannabis operations fell ~14% YoY to C$29.4M, while its liquor retail segment added C$104.1M with a ~5% YoY decline. The company attributed the underperformance to market headwinds in both the liquor and cannabis segments.

“Beyond the normal seasonality that impacts the first quarter each year, Q1 2026 was particularly challenging, driven primarily by market softness across our business segments and operating territories,” CEO Zach George remarked.

While its adjusted gross margin for the quarter slipped 60 bps to 27% and adjusted operating loss reached C$8.9M, largely steady from the prior-year period, SNDL’s GAAP earnings per share stood at -C$0.04, beating the consensus by C$0.01.

“While remaining focused on our strategic priorities and anticipating an improvement in the cannabis market in the second half of the year, we are not standing still,” CEO George added.

In addition to strategic adjustments in response to market softness, the company said that it has rolled out several initiatives that could generate nearly C$20M of additional operating income over the remaining quarter.

“We are proactively adjusting our commercial execution and cost structure to reflect the reality of current market conditions,” the CEO said.

data.title: Near the top of biotech rankings, AbbVie's Q1 beat makes a case for more upside
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) shares climbed roughly 2% in premarket trading Thursday after the pharma giant raised its full-year earnings outlook on the back of a strong first-quarter revenue beat, and the results reinforce why the company sits near the very top of Seeking Alpha's Quant Rankings for U.S. biotechnology stocks, holding a Buy rating of 4.25.

Below is a list of the top 10 biotechnology stocks with primary tickers in the United States, ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. The list features companies with diverse market capitalizations, ranging from approximately $2 billion to over $300 billion.

Within the biotech rankings, AbbVie's 4.25 Quant Rating places it joint second alongside Zymeworks (ZYME), just behind Relay Therapeutics (RLAY), which leads the list with a 4.82 Strong Buy.

Catalyst Pharmaceuticals (CPRX) is close behind at 4.23, while the remainder of the top 10 including Immunome (IMNM), Arcus Biosciences (RCUS), Exelixis (EXEL), Kymera Therapeutics (KYMR), Moderna (MRNA), and Krystal Biotech (KRYS), all carry Buy ratings between 3.60 and 3.95.

Notably, Moderna leads the group on year-to-date performance at +59.85%, with Relay Therapeutics not far behind at +54.02%. But it is AbbVie, with its scale, diversified portfolio, and raised guidance, that makes the most compelling fundamental case among the Buy-rated names on the list.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings system grades stocks on their relative performance across critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any rating of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating.

Here is the list:

  1. Relay Therapeutics, Inc. (RLAY), Quant Rating: 4.82

  2. AbbVie Inc. (ABBV), Quant Rating: 4.25

  3. Zymeworks Inc. (ZYME), Quant Rating: 4.25

  4. Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (CPRX), Quant Rating: 4.23

  5. Immunome, Inc. (IMNM), Quant Rating: 3.95

  6. Arcus Biosciences, Inc. (RCUS), Quant Rating: 3.94

  7. Exelixis, Inc. (EXEL), Quant Rating: 3.89

  8. Kymera Therapeutics, Inc. (KYMR), Quant Rating: 3.86

  9. Moderna, Inc. (MRNA), Quant Rating: 3.64

  10. Krystal Biotech, Inc. (KRYS), Quant Rating: 3.60

data.title: StoneCo cut to Neutral at Goldman Sachs ahead of Q1 earnings release
data.description:
StoneCo (STNE) was trading lower as Goldman Sachs cut its recommendation on the stock ahead of the first-quarter earnings release.

The Brazilian payment technology company is set to release its Q1 2026 financial results on Thursday, May 14, after the market closes.

Shares were 4.63% lower at $11.12 during morning trading on Wednesday.

"We downgrade shares of Stone to Neutral from Buy as we expect weaker earnings momentum (-1% yoy in 2026), given TPV (Total Payment Volume) underperformance relative to the industry and to main peer PAGS (PagSeguro Digital), leading to market share losses," said Goldman Sachs analysts.

"We note that PAGS screens more favorably during 2026 due to an easier comp base as it recovers from weak trends in 2025," added the research note.

"Moreover, while we acknowledge that STNE has de-rated this year (12-month fwd P/E falling 7% YTD), we see limited room for multiple expansion, given the deceleration in results (we see 6% downside to Visible Alpha Consensus 2026E net income)," said the research note.

"Furthermore, we note that STNE is now trading without one of its main catalysts: the R$3bn excess cash from the Linx divestiture (closed in February 2026), which will be distributed as extraordinary dividends on May 4th," said the research note.

Goldman Sachs' rating aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. The sell-side analysts as well as SA authors grade STNE as Buy.

data.title: Warner Bros. gains on report European antitrust regulator may clear Paramount Skydance deal
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) edged higher by 0.7% amid a report that European antitrust regulators are leaning toward approving the $110 billion sale to Paramount Skydance (PSKY). 

The European Commission appears to be skeptical of opponents' rationale against a transaction, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that was circulating on Wednesday. The  EC may clear the deal during its Phase 1 probe rather than from an in-depth inquiry of the deal.

The transaction is still in pre-notification phase in Europe and it's not clear when the filing will be made, though the companies are leaning towards late May or early June, according to the report.

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) expects to secure antitrust approvals comfortably in Europe to buy Warner Bros, (WBD) and would consider divestments if necessary, Reuters reported in late February.

Earlier this week, Paramount Skydance (PSKY)  flagged 49.5% foreign ownership to fund Warner Bros. *WBD) acquisition in an FCC filing.
